person.name: Maurice le Boteler
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»1229 Justice of Assize«/b»
Maurice Boteler was one of the justices of assize for the co. of Warwick in the 13th and 16th Henry III [1229 and 1232], and a commissioner for assessing and collecting the fourteenth part of all men's movable goods, according to the form and order then appointed. This feudal lord filled the office of justice of assize for the same shire a second and third time, and was repeatedly justice for the gaol delivery at Warwick in the same king's reign.

zh.Oversley Green, Warwickshire, England: 英國瓦維其可爾奧維斯利 格林
zh.Oversley, Warwickshire, England: 英國瓦維其可爾奧維斯利 格林
zh.lifeSummary: 當Maurice le Boteler於1185年出生在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾奧維斯利 格林時，他的父親Ralph II le Boteler60歲，母親Maud de Neufmarche35歲。他於1199年與Matilda de Bellamy在英國瓦維其可爾奧維斯利 格林結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1246年在英國英格蘭史洛普爾享年61歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maurice le Boteler于1185年出生在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾奧維斯利 格林时，他的父亲Ralph II le Boteler60岁，母亲Maud de Neufmarche35岁。他于1199年与Matilda de Bellamy在英国瓦維其可爾奧維斯利 格林结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1246年在英国英格兰史洛普爾享年61岁。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Boteler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker of leather bottles, from an agent derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bouteille ‘bottle’.

nameMeanings.name: Maurice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Late Latin name Mauricius, a derivative of Maurus (a byname meaning ‘Moor’, i.e. ‘dark, swarthy’), borne by, among others, an early Byzantine emperor ( c.539–602 ). It was introduced to Britain by the Normans and was popular in the Middle English period, but was not widely adopted by the nobility and became rare in the 17th century. Between the mid-19th century and the 1940s, it was moderately popular but has since faded again. See also Morris .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Maurice le Boteler was born in 1185, in Oversley Green, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Ralph II le Boteler, was 60 and his mother, Maud de Neufmarche, was 35. He married Matilda de Bellamy in 1199, in Oversley Green, Warwickshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He died in 1246, in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maurice le Boteler est né en 1185, à Oversley Green, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Ralph II le Boteler, avait 60 ans et sa mère, Maud de Neufmarche, avait 35 ans. Il a épousé Matilda de Bellamy en 1199, à Oversley Green, Warwickshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé en 1246, à Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 61 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maurice le Boteler 1185 in Oversley Green, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Ralph II le Boteler 60 und seine Mutter Maud de Neufmarche 35. Er hat 1199 in Oversley Green, Warwickshire, England Matilda de Bellamy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb 1246 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maurice le Boteler è nato il nel 1185 nel Oversley Green, Warwickshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Ralph II le Boteler, aveva 60 anni e il suo madre, Maud de Neufmarche, aveva 35 anni. Lui sposato Matilda de Bellamy nel 1199, nel Oversley Green, Warwickshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1246, nel Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 61 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Maurice le Botelerが1185年にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアオバースリー グレンで生まれた時，父Ralph II le Botelerは60歳，母Maud de Neufmarcheは35歳であった。1199年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアオバースリー グレンでMatilda de Bellamyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1246年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアで61歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Maurice le Boteler(이)가 1185년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 버슬리 그린에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ralph II le Boteler(은)는 60세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud de Neufmarche(은)는 35세였다. 그는 1199년 영국 워윅실르 버슬리 그린에서 Matilda de Bellamy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1246년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maurice le Boteler nasceu em 1185, em Oversley Green, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Ralph II le Boteler, tinha 60 anos e sua mãe, Maud de Neufmarche, tinha 35 anos. Ele casou-se com Matilda de Bellamy em 1199, em Oversley Green, Warwickshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1246, em Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 61 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maurice le Boteler родился в 1185 г. в местности овирсли грин, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Ralph II le Boteler было 60 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud de Neufmarche, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Matilda de Bellamy в 1199 г. в местности овирсли грин, варвиккшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер в 1246 г. в местности шропшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 61 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Maurice le Boteler nació en 1185, en Oversley Green, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Ralph II le Boteler, tenía 60 años y su mamá, Maud de Neufmarche, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Matilda de Bellamy en 1199, en Oversley Green, Warwickshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1246, en Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 61 años.
